mission.title: traefik: enable custom plugins/middlewares for Traefik
mission.description: enable custom plugins/middlewares for Traefik. Requested by 128+ users.

steps.title: Check current traefik deployment
steps.description:
Verify your traefik version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
helm list -n traefik 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working traefik installation.

steps.title: Review traefik configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant traefik configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
kubectl get configmap -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=traefik
```
After seeing the [Go1.8 new plugin feature](https://golang.org/pkg/plugin/) I though that this could help a lot o people to add specific functionalities to Traefik.

steps.title: Apply the fix for enable custom plugins/middlewares for Traefik
steps.description:
It's not that simple, trust me, we tried everything with go plugins. That's exactly what we started in https://github.com/containous/traefik/pull/1865. But at the end of the day, there were too many restrictions and really, you had to be lucky to make it work 😂 
Using multiple binaries [has also been explored](https://github.com/containous/traefik/pull/2362), but we found in our tests that there were too much overhead in performances. In middleware plugins, you cannot afford inter-process

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
kubectl get events -n traefik --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "enable custom plugins/middlewares for Traefik" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: It's not that simple, trust me, we tried everything with go plugins. That's exactly what we started in https://github.com/containous/traefik/pull/1865.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with traefik installed or the issue environment reproducible.
